parsed_review.summary: CP00-882 (RP29 Commercial Readiness UI Fixture Golden-Case Bridge, Risk C, 150 units, RP29.P04.M04.S16-RP29.P05.M02.S17) passes read-only review. The pack binding (registry.js:218-233), CP882 requirements, the live closeout-pack-plan.json entry, and the prompt baseline are mutually consistent: deliverable_distribution {fixture:25,test:8,hermes_evidence:7,claude_review:15,implementation:35,ui:48,security_audit:12} and micro_title_distribution both reconcile to 150, and required_section_rows derive correct per-micro counts. New implementation (ui.js createCommercialUiSurfaceMatrix/createCommercialUiFixtureGoldenCaseMatrix; registry.js CP882 descriptor/case-set/Hermes/Claude packets/handoff; validators.js UI-matrix + CP882 coverage/descriptor validators) enforces descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, no permission/audit writes, no runtime receipts, synthetic tenants/matters only, and no-count-leak across every row. Tests (model.test.js:415-465, service.test.js:150-190) assert zero validation errors for binding, coverage, descriptor, contract-current, compatibility-alias, and both UI matrices. The on-disk contract (commercial-readiness-contract.json) is consistent — current_pack/latest_pack CP00-882, projections.cp882 with ui_fixture_golden_case_matrix, required_capabilities/safety_gates/no_leak_guards present, validation.valid:true with no_write_attestation incl. local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is read-only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false), human approval gates any runtime opening, and Hermes evidence is receipt-free and not embedded in product runtime. No network/fs/exec/env access exists in the commercial package. The pack's eventual production_ready flag is a descriptor-verified claim (not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim) and is supported by static evidence. Review-evidence integrity for CP178+ is satisfied (auth preflight passed with valid JSON; no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed, or tool-shaped review evidence). No P0/P1 issues; two P3 provenance notes only.

findings.title: Verdict rests on static read-only inspection; full deterministic validation ladder not re-executed in-review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only two initial command runs (rp29:commercial:validate exit 0; node --test packages/commercial/test/*.test.js exit 0) and notes the full suite (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is 'required after review normalization ... and before commit.' As a read-only reviewer I cannot execute these commands; my PASS confirms the source/contract/plan/tests are internally consistent and should pass, not that the ladder was re-run during review.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the closeout step runs and records green results for the full validation ladder before promoting production_ready, and capture that command evidence in the pack.
findings.post_review_disposition: advisory_no_block — closeout gate already mandates the full ladder post-review; no code change required

findings.title: Pack closeout artifacts are in expected pending_review/production_ready:false pre-review state
findings.details: command-evidence.json (production_ready:false, gate_outcome:pending_pack_level_claude_review), construction-inspection.json (verdict:PENDING_REVIEW, claude_findings null), adjudication.md (all pending), and claude-review-result.json (status:pending_review, exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run:false, stdout_bytes:0) are all unpopulated. This is the correct pre-review baseline — these are promoted only after a valid review is normalized — but they must be updated with this review's outcome before closeout, and the 0-byte stdout here reflects pending state, not a malformed review.
findings.recommendation: Normalize this review into the receipt, adjudication, construction-inspection, and command-evidence artifacts (exactly one valid CP00-882 review) before flipping production_ready.
findings.post_review_disposition: expected_pre_review_state — normalize after this review; no defect in pack implementation
